title: Smart Home Hub
description: A full-stack IoT platform that connects and controls smart home devices through a responsive web dashboard.
longDescription: Smart Home Hub is a comprehensive IoT management platform built to bridge the gap between hardware sensors and user-friendly interfaces. The system integrates ESP32 microcontrollers, MQTT protocol, and a modern React dashboard to deliver real-time monitoring and control of home automation devices. Users can automate lighting, temperature, security cameras, and custom sensors through a clean drag-and-drop interface. The backend handles thousands of concurrent device connections using WebSocket streaming.
challenges: Handling thousands of concurrent MQTT device connections without latency spikes
Designing a real-time bidirectional sync between hardware and the web UI
Managing device state consistency when connections drop unexpectedly
solutions: Implemented a Node.js MQTT broker with connection pooling and message queuing for reliable delivery
Used WebSocket streams with React state reconciliation to keep UI in sync within 100ms
Designed a heartbeat protocol with automatic device reconnection and state recovery

title: E-Commerce Platform
description: A high-performance multi-vendor marketplace with real-time inventory, payment integration, and an admin analytics dashboard.
longDescription: A production-grade e-commerce platform built for multi-vendor businesses. The platform handles product listings, inventory management, order processing, and Stripe payment integration. An advanced admin dashboard provides real-time sales analytics, customer behavior tracking, and automated email campaigns. The architecture is designed for scale — Redis caching, CDN-optimized images, and server-side rendering give the storefront sub-second load times even during peak traffic.
challenges: Ensuring ACID compliance for concurrent order placements on the same inventory
Building a real-time inventory sync across multiple vendor storefronts
Optimizing Lighthouse scores to 95+ with dynamic product pages
solutions: Used PostgreSQL row-level locking and Prisma transactions to guarantee inventory integrity
Implemented Server-Sent Events (SSE) for live inventory broadcast across vendor dashboards
Adopted Next.js ISR with CDN caching + image optimization via next/image for performance

title: DevConnect
description: A real-time collaborative coding platform where developers can pair program, share code, and solve challenges together.
longDescription: DevConnect is a browser-based collaborative development environment designed for remote pair programming and technical interviews. It features a real-time code editor powered by operational transforms (OT), live cursor tracking, integrated video calling, and a code execution sandbox supporting 20+ languages. Teams can create private rooms, share snippets, and review session recordings. The platform is used by 500+ developers for technical interviews and coding bootcamp sessions.
challenges: Resolving edit conflicts in real-time when two users type simultaneously
Maintaining sub-50ms sync latency with up to 10 concurrent editors
Scaling WebRTC signaling for multiple video chat rooms
solutions: Implemented Operational Transformation (OT) algorithm for conflict-free collaborative editing
Used Redis pub/sub as a message bus between Node.js WebSocket servers for horizontal scaling
Built a TURN/STUN server setup with dynamic room management for WebRTC peer connections

title: 5-Axis Robotic Arm
description: A 3D-printed 5-axis robotic arm controlled by a custom web interface with real-time inverse kinematics calculations.
longDescription: A fully custom-built 5-axis robotic arm designed for educational and prototyping purposes. The mechanical structure is 3D-printed with PLA and driven by servo motors controlled via an Arduino Mega. A custom web dashboard allows users to control each joint individually or use the inverse kinematics (IK) mode to position the end-effector by specifying XYZ coordinates. The IK engine runs in JavaScript using a FABRIK algorithm for smooth, natural movement. The project was featured at a university innovation expo.
challenges: Achieving smooth, jitter-free servo movement across all 5 axes simultaneously
Implementing real-time IK computation without blocking the control loop
Calibrating joint angle limits to prevent mechanical damage
solutions: Implemented a PID controller with acceleration curves on each servo channel
Offloaded FABRIK IK calculations to a Web Worker thread for non-blocking execution
Built a software end-stop system with EEPROM-stored calibration profiles per arm configuration
